---
title: "Matrix_Full"
format: pdf
---

# General Question
We cluster the trends of measurements across time and group subjects with similar patterns across rows and columns.
U row covariance asks do measurements at occasions change together? (Across time)
V col covariance asks do measurements tend to change together? (Time flat)


# Matrix-Variate Normal Distribution

- n is the number of subjects
- r is the number of occasions (time points)
- p is the number of attributes

$$
\phi_{r\times p}(\mathbf{X} | \mathbf{M}, \mathbf{V}, \mathbf{U}) = \frac{1}{(2\pi)^{\frac{rp}{2}}|\mathbf{V}|^{\frac{r}{2}}|\mathbf{U}|^{\frac{p}{2}}} \exp \left\{-\frac{1}{2}\text{tr}\left(\mathbf{V}^{-1}(\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{M})^\top\mathbf{U}^{-1}(\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{M})\right) \right\}
$$

- M is a r x p mean matrix
- U is a r x r column covariance matrix
- V is a p x p column row covariance matrix

Note 1: that there is an identifiability issue with regard to U and V. If $k$ is a strictly positive constant, that replacing U and V by $(1/k)\mathbf{U}$ and $k\mathbf{V}$ respectively, leaves the distribution unchanged. Solutions have been proposed including setting $\text{tr}(\mathbf{U}) = r$ or $\mathbf{U}_{11} = 1$.

$$
\frac{1}{k}\mathbf{V} \otimes k\mathbf{U} = \mathbf{V} \otimes \mathbf{U}, \qquad \text{if} \ k >0
$$

Note 2: that the matrix-variate normal distribution is related to the multivariate normal distribution via the equivalence

$$
\mathbf{X} \sim \mathcal{N}_{r\times p} (\mathbf{M}, \mathbf{U}, \mathbf{V}) \iff \text{vec}({\mathbf{X}}) \sim \mathcal{N}_{rp}(\text{vec}(\mathbf{M}), \mathbf{V} \otimes \mathbf{U})
$$

## EM Updates

Let $z_{ig} = 1$ when observation $i$ belongs in group $g$, and $z_{ig} = 0$ else. Then, in the E-step we estimate the posterior $\hat{z}_{ig}$ that observation $\mathbf{X}_i$ belongs to group $g$

$$
\hat{z}_{ig} = \frac{\hat\pi_g\phi_{r\times p}\left(\mathbf{X}_i|\hat{\mathbf{M}}_g,\hat{\mathbf{V}}_g, \hat{\mathbf{U}}_g\right)}{\sum_{h=1}^G\pi_h\phi_{r\times p}\left(\mathbf{X}_i|\hat{\mathbf{M}}_h, \hat{\mathbf{V}}_h, \hat{\mathbf{U}}_h\right)}
$$

In M-step we get the maximum likelihood estimates:

$$
\begin{align}

\hat\pi_g &= \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig} \\

\hat{\mathbf{M}}_g &= \frac{1}{\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig}}\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig}\mathbf{X}_i \\

\hat{\mathbf{U}}_g &= \frac{1}{p\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig}}\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig}(\mathbf{X}_i - \hat{\mathbf{M}}_g)\hat{\mathbf{V}}_g^{-1}(\mathbf{X}_i - \hat{\mathbf{M}}_g)^\top \\

\hat{\mathbf{V}}_g &= \frac{1}{r\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig}}\sum_{i=1}^n\hat{z}_{ig}(\mathbf{X}_i - \hat{\mathbf{M}}_g)^\top\hat{\mathbf{U}}_g^{-1}(\mathbf{X}_i - \hat{\mathbf{M}}_g)

\end{align}
$$

We can initialize $\hat{z}_{ig}$ using k-means by vectorizing the matrices first. 

Meaning we define the Mahalonobis distance as

$$
\mathcal{D}_M(\mathbf{X}_i, \mathbf{M}, \mathbf{V}, \mathbf{U}) = \text{tr}\left\{\mathbf{U}^{-1}(\mathbf{X}_i - \mathbf{M})\mathbf{V^{-1}(\mathbf{X}_i - \mathbf{M})^\top} \right\}
$$

These will form as the foundation for our base Matrix Gaussian Mixture Model (MGMM) updates. Let us try putting that into a function!

## MGMM Implementation

```{r}
#| label: libraries
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

library(dplyr)
```

- `matrix_mahalanobis()` computes the matrix-variate Mahalanobis distance from one matrix to a component mean under the row and column covariance structure. It returns tr(U^{-1} (X - M) V^{-1} (X - M)^T).

- `matrix_mixture_kmeans_init()` builds the starting values for the mixture model using k-means on vectorized matrices. It returns initial mixing proportions, component mean matrices, row covariances, column covariances, and the starting cluster labels. 

```{r}
#| label: Mahalonbis_D
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Compute matrix-variate Mahalanobis distance
matrix_mahalanobis <- function(x, mean_matrix, row_cov, col_cov) {
	# U^{-1} and V^{-1}
	row_chol <- chol(row_cov)
	col_chol <- chol(col_cov)
	centered <- x - mean_matrix
	row_inv_centered <- backsolve(row_chol, forwardsolve(t(row_chol), centered))
	col_inv <- chol2inv(col_chol)

	sum(row_inv_centered * (centered %*% col_inv))
}

# kmeans initialization
matrix_mixture_kmeans_init <- function(x_list, g, nstart = 10) {
	# Extract dimensions
	n <- length(x_list)
	r <- nrow(x_list[[1]])
	p <- ncol(x_list[[1]])

	# vectorize and run kmeans for init
	x_matrix <- do.call(rbind, lapply(x_list, function(x) as.vector(x)))
	km <- kmeans(x_matrix, centers = g, nstart = nstart)
	z <- km$cluster

	mixing_proportions <- numeric(g)
	mean_matrices <- vector("list", g)
	row_covariances <- vector("list", g)
	col_covariances <- vector("list", g)

	# For each component, compute sample mean and covariances from k-means clusters
	for (component in seq_len(g)) {
		component_index <- which(z == component)
		if (length(component_index) == 0) {
			component_index <- sample.int(n, 1)  # Fallback if k-means created empty cluster
		}

		component_data <- x_list[component_index]
		mixing_proportions[component] <- length(component_index) / n
		# Calculates M_hat
		mean_matrices[[component]] <- Reduce(`+`, component_data) / length(component_data)

		# M-step for the covariance U and V
		row_cov <- matrix(0, r, r)
		col_cov <- matrix(0, p, p)
		for (x in component_data) {
			centered <- x - mean_matrices[[component]]
			row_cov <- row_cov + centered %*% t(centered)
			col_cov <- col_cov + t(centered) %*% centered
		}
		
		row_cov <- row_cov / (p * length(component_data))
		col_cov <- col_cov / (r * length(component_data))

		# Enforce identifiability scale so that tr(U) = r
		row_covariances[[component]] <- row_cov
		col_covariances[[component]] <- col_cov
		row_scale <- r / sum(diag(row_covariances[[component]]))
		row_covariances[[component]] <- row_covariances[[component]] * row_scale
		col_covariances[[component]] <- col_covariances[[component]] / row_scale
	}

	list(
		pi = mixing_proportions,
		M = mean_matrices,
		U = row_covariances,
		V = col_covariances,
		cluster = z
	)
}
```

- `matrix_variate_log_density()` (Z_igs posterior E-step Update) computes the log-density of a single matrix under the matrix normal distribution. It uses the row covariance and column covariance separately.

- `matrix_variate_mixture_fit()` is the main EM fitter for the matrix variate Gaussian mixture model. It repeatedly does the E-step to compute responsibilities and the M-step to update mixing proportions, means, row covariances, and column covariances until convergence or the iteration limit is reached.

```{r}
#| label: MGMM_base
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Compute log-density of single matrix under matrix-variate normal
matrix_variate_log_density <- function(x, mean_matrix, row_cov, col_cov) {
	# Cholesky decomposition
	row_chol <- chol(row_cov)
	col_chol <- chol(col_cov)

	# |U| and |V| for the denominator
	row_logdet <- 2 * sum(log(diag(row_chol)))
	col_logdet <- 2 * sum(log(diag(col_chol)))

	# tr(V^{-1} * (X - M)^T * U^{-1} * (X - M))
	centered <- x - mean_matrix
	row_inv_centered <- backsolve(row_chol, forwardsolve(t(row_chol), centered))
	col_inv <- chol2inv(col_chol)
	trace_form <- sum(row_inv_centered * (centered %*% col_inv))

	r <- nrow(x)
	p <- ncol(x)

	# Returns log density value
	-0.5 * (r * p * log(2 * pi) + p * row_logdet + r * col_logdet + trace_form)
}

matrix_variate_mixture_fit <- function(x_list, g, max_iter = 100, tol = 1e-06,
																			 nstart = 10, verbose = FALSE) {
	if (!is.list(x_list) || length(x_list) == 0) {
		stop("x_list must be a non-empty list of matrices.")
	}

	n <- length(x_list)
	r <- nrow(x_list[[1]])
	p <- ncol(x_list[[1]])

	for (x in x_list) {
		if (!is.matrix(x) || nrow(x) != r || ncol(x) != p) {
			stop("All elements of x_list must be matrices with the same dimensions.")
		}
	}

	# Initialize parameters using k-means
	params <- matrix_mixture_kmeans_init(x_list, g = g, nstart = nstart)
	loglik_trace <- numeric(0)
	responsibilities <- matrix(0, n, g)  # Will hold posterior probabilities P(z_ig = 1 | X_i)

	log_sum_exp <- function(values) {
		max_value <- max(values)
		max_value + log(sum(exp(values - max_value)))
	}

	# EM loop
	for (iteration in seq_len(max_iter)) {
		# E-step
		log_density <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = g)

		for (component in seq_len(g)) {
			for (i in seq_len(n)) {
				# compute P(X_i | component)
				log_density[i, component] <- log(params$pi[component]) +
					matrix_variate_log_density(
						x = x_list[[i]],
						mean_matrix = params$M[[component]],
						row_cov = params$U[[component]],
						col_cov = params$V[[component]]
					)
			}
		}

		# Normalize log-densities to get responsibilities
		for (i in seq_len(n)) {
			row_log_densities <- log_density[i, ]
			normalizer <- log_sum_exp(row_log_densities)  # numerically stable log-sum-exp
			responsibilities[i, ] <- exp(row_log_densities - normalizer)  # z_hat_ig
		}

		# Compute observed data log-likelihood for convergence check
		current_loglik <- sum(apply(log_density, 1, log_sum_exp))
		loglik_trace <- c(loglik_trace, current_loglik)

		# Check convergence
		if (iteration > 1 && abs(loglik_trace[iteration] - loglik_trace[iteration - 1]) < tol) {
			break
		}

		# M-step
		component_sizes <- colSums(responsibilities)  # sum of z_hat_ig over all observations i
		new_params <- params

		# Update each component's parameters
		for (component in seq_len(g)) {
			if (component_sizes[component] <= 0) {
				next
			}

			weights <- responsibilities[, component]
			weights_sum <- component_sizes[component]  # effective sample size for this component

			# Update mean matrix (M-step: M_hat_g)
			mean_matrix <- matrix(0, r, p)
			for (i in seq_len(n)) {
				mean_matrix <- mean_matrix + weights[i] * x_list[[i]]
			}
			mean_matrix <- mean_matrix / weights_sum

			# Update row covariance U_hat_g
			row_cov <- matrix(0, r, r)
			for (i in seq_len(n)) {
				centered <- x_list[[i]] - mean_matrix
				row_cov <- row_cov + weights[i] * (centered %*% solve(params$V[[component]], t(centered)))
			}
			row_cov <- row_cov / (p * weights_sum)
			
			# Enforce tr(U) = r
			row_scale <- r / sum(diag(row_cov))
			row_cov <- row_cov * row_scale

			# Update column covariance V_hat_g
			col_cov <- matrix(0, p, p)
			for (i in seq_len(n)) {
				centered <- x_list[[i]] - mean_matrix
				col_cov <- col_cov + weights[i] * (t(centered) %*% solve(row_cov, centered))
			}
			col_cov <- col_cov / (r * weights_sum)

			# Store updated parameters
			new_params$pi[component] <- weights_sum / n  # mixing proportion
			new_params$M[[component]] <- mean_matrix
			new_params$U[[component]] <- row_cov
			new_params$V[[component]] <- col_cov
		}

		# Normalize mixing proportions to sum to 1
		new_params$pi <- new_params$pi / sum(new_params$pi)

		params <- new_params

		if (verbose) {
			message(sprintf("Iteration %d: log-likelihood = %.4f", iteration, current_loglik))
		}
	}

	# Assign each observation to its most likely component
	cluster_membership <- max.col(responsibilities, ties.method = "first")

	# Return fitted model with all parameters and diagnostics
	list(
		pi = params$pi,
		M = params$M,
		U = params$U,
		V = params$V,
		z = responsibilities,
		cluster = cluster_membership,
		logLik = loglik_trace,
		iterations = length(loglik_trace),
		converged = length(loglik_trace) < max_iter
	)
}

```

```{r}
#| label: fit_ex
#| warning: false
#| message: false

set.seed(123)

simulate_matrix_group <- function(n, mean_matrix, row_sd = 0.35, col_sd = 0.35) {
	r <- nrow(mean_matrix)
	p <- ncol(mean_matrix)

	row_cov <- diag(row_sd, r)
	col_cov <- diag(col_sd, p)

	lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {
		noise <- matrix(rnorm(r * p), r, p)
		mean_matrix + row_cov %*% noise %*% col_cov
	})
}

mean_1 <- matrix(c(1.5, 1.2, 1.0, 1.3,
					 1.1, 1.4, 1.2, 1.0), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
mean_2 <- matrix(c(-1.4, -1.0, -1.2, -1.3,
					 -1.1, -1.5, -1.0, -1.2), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)

x_list <- c(
	simulate_matrix_group(n = 15, mean_matrix = mean_1),
	simulate_matrix_group(n = 15, mean_matrix = mean_2)
)

fit <- matrix_variate_mixture_fit(x_list = x_list, g = 2, max_iter = 50, verbose = TRUE)

fit$cluster
fit$pi
fit$logLik

# Quick diagnostics:
# 1. The log-likelihood should generally increase across iterations.
# 2. The fitted clusters should separate the two simulated groups.
# 3. The component mean matrices should reflect the positive/negative pattern we simulated.

true_labels <- rep(1:2, each = 15)
pred_labels <- fit$cluster

accuracy_1 <- mean(pred_labels == true_labels)
accuracy_2 <- mean((3 - pred_labels) == true_labels)
best_accuracy <- max(accuracy_1, accuracy_2)
aligned_labels <- if (accuracy_2 > accuracy_1) 3 - pred_labels else pred_labels

best_accuracy
table(True = true_labels, Predicted = aligned_labels)
```
